sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः । तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु जिन्होंने अपने जिस ज्ञानविवक के द्वारा उस अज्ञानका नाश कर दिया है, उनका वह ज्ञान सूर्यकी तरह परमतत्त्व परमात्माको प्रकाशित कर देता है ॥
englishTranslation: But for those whose ignorance has been destroyed by knowledge of the true self, that knowledge reveals the supreme reality like the sun.
essence: Clear seeing does not create reality; it uncovers what was always there.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: When ignorance is removed by true understanding, that understanding itself reveals the highest reality, just as the sun reveals everything in daylight.
simpleInsight: Clarity is not added from outside; once confusion falls away, what is real becomes obvious.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains why people act blindly. Here Krishna says that when a person’s ignorance is destroyed by insight, the highest reality becomes visible at once, like sunlight after darkness.
modernRelevance: You reread the same message ten times, then suddenly understand the room was never the problem. The facts were there; confusion was filtering them. Clear seeing changes everything.
translationLiteral: ज्ञानेन (by knowledge) / तु (but) / तत्-अज्ञानम् (that ignorance) / येषाम् (of those whose) / नाशितम् (destroyed) / आत्मनः (of the true self) / तेषाम् (for them) / आदित्य-वत् (like the sun) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / प्रकाशयति (reveals) / तत्-परम् (the supreme reality)
